shared_properties: Gauge occupies a 240x8 px bar centered near the bottom HUD edge so it remains visible without obscuring the shaft lane.
Core palette metadata remains primary #3B3347, background #0D0D0F, and accent #E07B39 while friction zone colors stay inside specs.colors_hex.
Left, center, and right segments stay symmetrical so the player can read one-sided and dual braking states instantly.
differences: State sheet includes left_brake, dual_brake, neutral, right_brake, and slick states rather than a single static bar.
High-friction segments use #3B3347, medium support markers use #4E5940, and low-friction or free-fall cues use #4A90A4.
A 4 px amber indicator notch shifts among authored positions so input direction is readable even during fast descent.

reference_images.label: gdd_markdown
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the 240x8 bottom-center friction gauge, left-right input mapping, and no-extra-progress-UI rule described by the GDD.

reference_images.label: moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the cold dungeon palette so the gauge can echo friction materials without feeling like a modern neon UI.

reference_images.label: player_concept
reference_images.purpose: Grounds player-readable control feedback for the no-jump descent mechanic and dual-brake minimum speed state.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: ImageBundle for static HUD art and SpriteSheetBundle for multi-state overlays and gauges that swap atlas indices.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load UI textures during the shared Loading state and store handles in a HudVisualRegistry keyed by asset_key so gameplay systems never parse filenames.
runtime_notes: Anchor UI nodes to whole pixels in the 1280x720 output space while the gameplay layer is upscaled from 320x180.
Keep the friction gauge, HP strip, and timer on a dedicated HUD layer above parallax and hazard sprites.
Swap overlay states by atlas index or visibility flags instead of rebuilding UI trees during pause, fail, or clear transitions.

context.usage: Bottom-center mechanic readout for friction intensity and directional contact.

acceptance_criteria: State sheet is exactly 1200x8 px with 5 frames of 240x8 px and 0 px spacing.
Each frame preserves 30 segments of 8 px width and a 4 px amber indicator notch at a deterministic authored position.
High-friction and low-friction zones remain visually distinct through #3B3347 versus #4A90A4 dominance.
